chunk_summary: This section discusses energy and protein targets in pediatric CKD diets, emphasizing lab-linked restrictions for hyperkalemia and growth protection while avoiding blanket nutrient restrictions.
learning_objectives: Understand energy intake targets for pediatric CKD based on age and growth needs.
Learn appropriate protein intake guidelines for children with CKD, including cases of inflammation or dialysis.
Identify practical potassium restriction thresholds and techniques for managing hyperkalemia.
Recognize the importance of monitoring growth trajectory and lab markers in pediatric CKD management.
key_facts: Energy intake should target approximately 100% of estimated energy requirements for age, with adjustments for growth failure or catabolism.
Protein intake should meet or exceed age-based recommendations, with higher targets for children with poor growth, inflammation, or on dialysis.
Potassium restriction is recommended for persistent hyperkalemia, defined as serum potassium ≥5.5 mmol/L despite addressing underlying causes.
Techniques for potassium reduction include leaching high-potassium vegetables and avoiding potassium-containing salt substitutes.
Phosphate restriction should focus on reducing additives and ultra-processed foods before limiting natural protein sources.
reference_quote: Routine blanket restriction of protein or potassium commonly causes undernutrition without improving outcomes. (Source: https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC6968982/)
document_key_topics: Energy intake targets in pediatric CKD
Protein requirements for growth and inflammation
